title: Darwis's Portfolio Website
description:
A clean and modern personal portfolio built with Next.js, featuring smooth, dynamic animations using Framer Motion.
This website serves as a personal introduction — showcasing who he is, what drives him, and the technical skills he's developed over time. It also features a curated selection of projects to demonstrate his capabilities. To top it off, there's a dedicated contact section for collaborations, job opportunities, or friendly conversations. The overall experience is designed to be intuitive, visually appealing, and easy to navigate.
author: Darwis Shathibi

title: DeTA Community
description: DETA"Developer Community by Professionals and Students" is a platform focused on empowering coding skills through collaboration, mentorship, and hands-on projects. It brings together experienced professionals and aspiring developers to learn, build, and grow in a supportive community.
author: Developer Tanah Air

title: DeTA Meetup
description: DeTA Meetup is a place to be an awesome developer. 🚀 Connect with fellow developers and creators, engage in hands-on activities, and explore exciting networking opportunities. Showcase your projects, exchange ideas, and be part of a thriving tech community!
author: Developer Tanah Air

title: Forthify Technologies
description: Tech startup established in IIUM, based in Gombak, powered by a dedicated community. Focusing on modern and state-of-the-art web application solutions to build visually stunning and impactful software.
author: Forthify

title: ICE Community
description: ICE Community is a student-led cybersecurity initiative dedicated to empowering university clubs in Malaysia. Our mission is to foster a strong, collaborative, and approachable cybersecurity community by providing educational resources, hands-on challenges, and networking opportunities. We aim to elevate student efforts, bridge the gap between academia and industry, and cultivate the next generation of cybersecurity professionals.
author: ICE

title: ICECTF
description: ICECTF is a co-organize event between ICE and SIG Cybersecurity IIUM. The Capture The Flag(CTF) competition is a cybersecurity challenge where participants solve security-related puzzles or challenges to find "flags," which are hidden strings or pieces of information.
author: ICE x SIG

title: IRC Website
description: The IIUM Robotic Competition (IRC) is an annual international robotics event organized by IIUM Roboteam and Makers@IIUM. It provides a platform for primary and secondary students to showcase their robotics talents while promoting innovation, teamwork, and unity among young participants from various schools and countries.
author: Forthify

title: Modern Full-Stack Hero Workshop
description: The Modern Full-Stack Hero Workshop equips participants with essential skills in frontend and backend development using React, Next.js, Supabase, and more. Through hands-on projects, mentorship, and real-world tools, learners build and deploy a full-stack web app, earning certification and portfolio-ready experience for future tech opportunities.
author: Developer Tanah Air

title: Motion-U
description: The Motion-U Club fosters a vibrant community where technology enthusiasts converge to innovate and embark on entrepreneurial journeys. Our activities such as workshop and talk, bootcamp, hands-on project, competition and study group can help members to explore the intersection of technology and entrepreneurship, equipped with skills and networks to shape the future of innovation and make meaningful impact.
author: Motion-U 24/25

title: Multy
description: Multy is an all-in-one event management hub that allows you to upload your event, create a dedicated landing page, and seamlessly share it with other communities. Powered by a collaboration with Pro Reg, Multy simplifies promotion and participant registration for organizers.
author: Deta Community

title: ProReg
description: Calendar management system for university students.!
author: Forthify

title: que-r
description: Traditional QR codes are static—once printed, they can't be changed.
author: Deta Community
